summary: Removed deprecated tooling from the repository, deleting 17 files and 1575 lines with no additions. The cleanup eliminated the old calcprogress Python package, elf2dol.c and its Makefile build rule, plus standalone helper scripts link.py, report_score.py, and revert_asm.py. There were no PR body notes or review comments in the provided slice, so the rationale is inferred mainly from the title and all-deletion diff.
what_changed: Deleted tools/elf2dol.c, a C utility previously built by tools/Makefile.
Deleted tools/Makefile, which only compiled elf2dol from elf2dol.c using cc with -O3 -Wall -s and provided a clean target.
Removed the entire tools/calcprogress subtree, including README, license, local git metadata files, entrypoint script, and src modules.
Removed calcprogress support code for parsing CodeWarrior maps, DOL/REL data, object lists, assembly section directives, input streams, and progress calculations.
Removed standalone Python helper scripts tools/link.py, tools/report_score.py, and tools/revert_asm.py.
The diff contains only deletions: 0 added lines and 1575 deleted lines across 17 files.
smart_moves: The cleanup removed obsolete tools wholesale instead of leaving partial entrypoints, local Makefile targets, or documentation for utilities no longer intended to be used.

Deleting the entire calcprogress directory avoided preserving stale README guidance about CodeWarrior/devkitPPC progress calculation, custom slice tracking, DOL/REL progress callbacks, and asm/object extension options.
Removing tools/Makefile together with elf2dol.c prevented a dangling build recipe for a deleted utility.
The change was narrowly scoped to deprecated tools and did not mix source decompilation changes with repository maintenance.
decomp_lessons: Keep the tools directory aligned with the current decomp workflow; deprecated utilities can create false affordances and maintenance burden if left in-tree.
When removing a deprecated tool, remove its documentation, local ignore/attribute files, license file if only attached to that subtree, and build hooks in the same PR.
Progress-reporting infrastructure can age out as project workflows change; stale progress calculators may be more confusing than helpful for future contributors.
This PR provides no direct matching, struct, or symbol-decomp technique; its relevance is repository hygiene and toolchain simplification.
naming_conventions: Deleted tool names used lowercase snake_case or compact lowercase forms: calcprogress, elf2dol, report_score.py, revert_asm.py, link.py.
Deleted calcprogress internals used descriptive module names such as asm_util.py, cw_map.py, dol.py, input_stream.py, find_asm_functions.py, and progress.py.
No new naming convention was introduced by this PR.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly matching tactic was introduced; the PR only removed deprecated tools.

The removed calcprogress documentation described an old progress-estimation tactic: read total code/data size from the DOL, parse CodeWarrior map section header symbols, parse obj_files.mk to identify linked assembly files, scan assembly for .section directives, and sum remaining assembly section sizes.
The removed calcprogress code supported custom assembly source/object extensions such as .s and .o, and had workarounds for projects where assembly and source object files shared names.
The removed map parser had compatibility paths for old and new CodeWarrior map formats, but there is no evidence in the PR that this logic should continue to be reused.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale or requested changes were available.
follow_up_queries: Check later tooling PRs for the replacement progress-reporting workflow after calcprogress was removed.
Search for references to elf2dol, calcprogress, report_score.py, revert_asm.py, or link.py in scripts and documentation to ensure no stale references remained.
Look for CI or build-system changes around this date that explain why tools/elf2dol.c and tools/Makefile were no longer needed.
